summary.intent: Address Codex review findings on PR #694 for the RatingGroup component, fixing accessibility and behavioral bugs while maintaining or improving the 90% branch coverage threshold.
summary.outcome: All four Codex findings were resolved (aria-labelledby conditionality, aria-required on visible control, controlled release value fix, threshold restored to 90%), the wasm-bindgen runner version mismatch was fixed, and full coverage passed at exactly 90% branch coverage before pushing the commit.

repo: The repo uses `cargo xtask ci coverage` which runs both native and wasm coverage legs, then merges LCOV files and checks per-crate thresholds — all legs must pass before the gate is satisfied.
Coverage threshold for ars-components is 90% branch coverage, enforced exactly (not rounded) — being at 90.0% display can still fail if the raw fraction is slightly below 0.9.
wasm coverage requires `wasm-bindgen-test-runner` to exactly match the version in Cargo.lock (0.2.122); a mismatch causes the wasm leg to fail entirely.
Codex review is triggered via `@codex review` PR comment; reactions (+1) from the connector indicate a completed review cycle.
The repo has a `.agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md` skill that governs the workflow for handling Codex review cycles.
Snapshots are managed via `cargo insta accept` and need to be updated when intentional HTML attribute changes are made (e.g., removing unconditional aria-labelledby).

code.finding: aria-labelledby was unconditionally emitted even when no adapter label was provided; fix makes it conditional on label presence.
aria-required was only placed on the hidden input, not the visible control — fixed to apply to the visible element too.
Controlled release was reading the old controlled value instead of the pending requested value — behavioral bug in value handling during release.
Root disabled/readonly attribute branches were uncovered; adding assertions for guard interactions was the marginal branch hit needed to cross the exact 90% threshold.
Coverage threshold comparison is exact floating-point, not display-rounded — `6469/7188 = 89.9971...%` fails even though it prints as 90.0%.

workflow: When a full coverage pipeline fails on toolchain mismatch (wasm-bindgen version), install the exact locked version via `cargo install wasm-bindgen-cli --locked --version <exact>`.
To find marginal branch coverage gaps, parse the merged LCOV with awk filtering for `BRDA` lines where hit count is 0, then map back to source line numbers.
Merging LCOV files manually with `cargo xtask coverage merge` + `cargo xtask coverage check` allows partial verification without rerunning the full pipeline.
Committing review fixes with `Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>` is the convention for attributing Codex-triggered changes in this repo.

friction: wasm-bindgen-test-runner version mismatch (0.2.121 installed vs 0.2.122 required) caused a full pipeline failure that required manual detection and `cargo install` fix.

The full `cargo xtask ci coverage` pipeline is very long-running (compiles native + multiple wasm targets, runs 6787+ tests) — had to run it twice due to the toolchain issue and once more for the branch fix.
Branch coverage failure was one hit short but displayed as 90.0% due to floating-point display rounding, making it non-obvious why the gate failed.
The `cargo xtask coverage merge` subcommand CLI syntax was unclear — required trial and error to find the correct argument format.
open_items: The wasm-bindgen version mismatch is an environment issue that could recur for other contributors — consider documenting the required version or adding a setup script check.

The exact floating-point coverage threshold comparison in xtask/src/coverage.rs may cause intermittent friction as coverage naturally fluctuates near the boundary — consider whether a small epsilon tolerance is appropriate.
